items.label: content

items.text: Within this framework, automation enables capital to replace labor in tasks it was previously engaged in.
items.label: content

items.text: This process shifts the task content of production against labor because of a displacement effect.
items.label: content

items.text: However, the effects of automation are counterbalanced by the creation of new tasks in which labor has a comparative advantage.
items.label: content

items.text: The introduction of new tasks changes the task content of production in favor of labor because of a reinstatement effect.
items.label: content

items.text: This reinstatement effect always raises the labor share and labor demand.
items.label: content

items.text: In contrast to the displacement effect, which shifts the task content of production against labor, the reinstatement effect changes the task content of production in favor of labor.
items.label: content

items.text: Whereas the displacement effect reflects capital replacing labor in existing tasks, the reinstatement effect arises from the creation of new tasks in which labor has a comparative advantage.
items.label: content

items.text: The authors provide an empirical decomposition to assess these dynamics in practice.

items.label: content

items.text: By comparison, a weaker reinstatement effect has also contributed to the observed employment trends.
items.label: content

items.text: Although the reinstatement effect always raises the labor share and labor demand, the empirical evidence indicates that a weaker reinstatement effect has contributed to the slower growth of employment over this period.
